item.title: Battlesector & Gladius will take part in Warhammer Skulls Festival
item.description:
We’re excited to announce that Warhammer 40,000: Battlesector and Warhammer 40,000: Gladius will be taking part in Warhammer Skulls 2026!

The Skulls festival will run from Thursday, May 21st, at 5:00 PM UK until Thursday, May 28th.

Don’t miss the live showcase on the official Warhammer Twitch channel on Thursday, May 21st at 5:00 PM UK time.

Stay tuned, and discover more about Skulls here.
item.content:encoded:
We’re excited to announce that Warhammer 40,000: Battlesector and Warhammer 40,000: Gladius will be taking part in Warhammer Skulls 2026!

The Skulls festival will run from Thursday, May 21st, at 5:00 PM UK until Thursday, May 28th.

Don’t miss the live showcase on the official Warhammer Twitch channel on Thursday, May 21st at 5:00 PM UK time.

Stay tuned, and discover more about Skulls here.

item.title: Bhashiva Character Pack - Faction Overview
item.description:
Hey folks!

The Bhashiva Character Pack introduces Bhashiva, The White Tiger, as a new playable Legendary Lord alongside deadly new Tiger Warrior units for the armies of Grand Cathay. 



The Bhashiva Character Pack is available May 21st 📅

— The Total War Team

For the latest news and announcements, don't forget to visit our official social channels. Steam X Facebook Instagram Discord [url=https://bsky.app/profile/totalwar.bsky.social style=button]Bluesky[/url]
item.content:encoded:
Hey folks!

The Bhashiva Character Pack introduces Bhashiva, The White Tiger, as a new playable Legendary Lord alongside deadly new Tiger Warrior units for the armies of Grand Cathay. 



The Bhashiva Character Pack is available May 21st 📅

— The Total War Team

For the latest news and announcements, don't forget to visit our official social channels. Steam X Facebook Instagram Discord [url=https://bsky.app/profile/totalwar.bsky.social style=button]Bluesky[/url]

item.title: Mechanicus II Launches Next Week!
item.description:
Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus II will officially launch on May 21st, 2026, coinciding with the start of this year’s Warhammer Skulls Festival.

Since our first reveal in Warhammer Skulls two years ago, and right up to our demo last October, we’ve been incredibly grateful for all the feedback, excitement, theories and patience from our community. We’ve been hard at work refining and expanding the experience, and we cannot wait to finally place it in your hands.

To celebrate the announcement, we’ve released a new leader deep-dive video to introduce you to two more key characters in the war to come: Lector-Dogmatis Videx, a zealous preacher of the Machine God’s orthodoxy, and Lord Obasis, the Sankhotep dynasty’s implacable bulwark against disorder.

Whether you’re terraforming new ground in the name of the Omnissiah, or awakening to reclaim a domain that was once yours, the bitter war for the world of Hekateus IV begins on May 21st.

Wishlist now and join us in our community Discord for all the latest news and discussions.
item.content:encoded:
Warhammer 40,000: Mechanicus II will officially launch on May 21st, 2026, coinciding with the start of this year’s Warhammer Skulls Festival.

Since our first reveal in Warhammer Skulls two years ago, and right up to our demo last October, we’ve been incredibly grateful for all the feedback, excitement, theories and patience from our community. We’ve been hard at work refining and expanding the experience, and we cannot wait to finally place it in your hands.

To celebrate the announcement, we’ve released a new leader deep-dive video to introduce you to two more key characters in the war to come: Lector-Dogmatis Videx, a zealous preacher of the Machine God’s orthodoxy, and Lord Obasis, the Sankhotep dynasty’s implacable bulwark against disorder.

Whether you’re terraforming new ground in the name of the Omnissiah, or awakening to reclaim a domain that was once yours, the bitter war for the world of Hekateus IV begins on May 21st.

Wishlist now and join us in our community Discord for all the latest news and discussions.

item.title: More War: Vlad von Carstein Let's Play
item.description:
It's Vlad's turn for the More War treatment 🧛 I guess he'll rampage through the Empire, raise some zombie armies, kill Karl Franz and raze Altdorf, right? WRONG!



— The Total War Team

For the latest news and announcements, don't forget to visit our official social channels. Steam X Facebook Instagram Discord [url=https://bsky.app/profile/totalwar.bsky.social style=button]Bluesky[/url]
item.content:encoded:
It's Vlad's turn for the More War treatment 🧛 I guess he'll rampage through the Empire, raise some zombie armies, kill Karl Franz and raze Altdorf, right? WRONG!



— The Total War Team

For the latest news and announcements, don't forget to visit our official social channels. Steam X Facebook Instagram Discord [url=https://bsky.app/profile/totalwar.bsky.social style=button]Bluesky[/url]

item.title: Eogunn Februs: Genetor Extremis
item.description:
In the grim darkness of the 41st millennium, everyone has a function to fulfil, working away and sacrificing their lives so that the machine that is the Imperium keeps on running and humanity can survive amid the merciless stars. The Adeptus Mechanicus is one of the pillars of the Imperium and the knowledge its adepts gather and preserve in the name of the Machine God they worship is critical to the Imperium's continued operation.

Among its Tech-Priests, there are those who, besides being experts in arcane blessed knowledge and sacred machines, are versed in the mysteries of genetics, alchemy, and organics. Genetors probe the mysteries of the biological, creating strange cybernetic creature-amalgams and slaughtering xenos beyond number to uncover their alien frailties. The Quest for Knowledge leads many of them to search for the Machine God's revelations in the remotest and most mysterious corners of the Galaxy.

Eogunn Februs is a Genetor Extremis of unmatched proficiency in the art of augmetics and the methods of integrating implants with flesh. With a keen and restless intellect, he spends his time experimenting and contemplating. He works ceaselessly to discover a way to extend the functioning of the human body — whatever the cost. As a Tech-Priest Manipulus, he also wields the Motive Force via a galvanic cell, from which they channel powerful charges of The Motive Force. They drain power sources dry and use the energy to overcharge the cells of their warriors’ armaments, all while defending their divine work with blasts from their own arcane weapons.

Genetor Februs's manners are fairly eccentric, his speech esoteric, and his experiments often horrifying in the eyes of laypeople. He firmly believes that the xenos’s own technology can be turned against them and therefore doesn't shun its study, in spite of many believing this to be heretical and blasphemous.

But what is the price of touching things forbidden, and what sacrifices must be made in the name of reaching one’s goal? Confronting the unfathomable technological terror awakening in the Koronus Expanse will drive Eogunn's thirst for knowledge to extremes, and take him to the very threshold between genius and madness.
item.content:encoded:
In the grim darkness of the 41st millennium, everyone has a function to fulfil, working away and sacrificing their lives so that the machine that is the Imperium keeps on running and humanity can survive amid the merciless stars. The Adeptus Mechanicus is one of the pillars of the Imperium and the knowledge its adepts gather and preserve in the name of the Machine God they worship is critical to the Imperium's continued operation.

Among its Tech-Priests, there are those who, besides being experts in arcane blessed knowledge and sacred machines, are versed in the mysteries of genetics, alchemy, and organics. Genetors probe the mysteries of the biological, creating strange cybernetic creature-amalgams and slaughtering xenos beyond number to uncover their alien frailties. The Quest for Knowledge leads many of them to search for the Machine God's revelations in the remotest and most mysterious corners of the Galaxy.

Eogunn Februs is a Genetor Extremis of unmatched proficiency in the art of augmetics and the methods of integrating implants with flesh. With a keen and restless intellect, he spends his time experimenting and contemplating. He works ceaselessly to discover a way to extend the functioning of the human body — whatever the cost. As a Tech-Priest Manipulus, he also wields the Motive Force via a galvanic cell, from which they channel powerful charges of The Motive Force. They drain power sources dry and use the energy to overcharge the cells of their warriors’ armaments, all while defending their divine work with blasts from their own arcane weapons.

Genetor Februs's manners are fairly eccentric, his speech esoteric, and his experiments often horrifying in the eyes of laypeople. He firmly believes that the xenos’s own technology can be turned against them and therefore doesn't shun its study, in spite of many believing this to be heretical and blasphemous.

But what is the price of touching things forbidden, and what sacrifices must be made in the name of reaching one’s goal? Confronting the unfathomable technological terror awakening in the Koronus Expanse will drive Eogunn's thirst for knowledge to extremes, and take him to the very threshold between genius and madness.
